text.title: TTS Basics — Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every single-call TTS application must enforce. Source: OpenAI TTS docs, ElevenLabs ToS, Google Cloud TTS SSML guide, production replay logs.

rule.statement: Use `tts-1` for draft and preview synthesis; promote to `tts-1-hd` only for final production assets. `tts-1-hd` adds roughly 2x latency and 2x cost ($0.030 vs $0.015 per 1k chars).

rationale.source: OpenAI TTS pricing page; internal iteration replays

rule.rationale: The quality gain of `tts-1-hd` is audible only in final delivery. Using it during iteration doubles both wall-clock and dollars without measurable downstream benefit.

rule.statement: Route any payload containing SSML markup (`<break>`, `<prosody>`, `<say-as>`, `<phoneme>`) to Google Cloud TTS or Azure Cognitive Services only. Never pass SSML to OpenAI TTS or ElevenLabs.

rationale.source: OpenAI TTS API docs (no SSML support); Google Cloud TTS SSML reference

rule.rationale: OpenAI TTS reads SSML tags verbatim as literal text — the listener hears angle brackets, attribute names, and quotes. ElevenLabs ignores SSML silently. Only Google and Azure parse SSML as pronunciation control.

rule.statement: Cache key MUST be `sha256(text + "|" + voice + "|" + str(speed) + "|" + model + "|" + provider)`. Provider in the key is non-optional; identical text+voice produces different audio across providers.

rationale.source: Cache invariance proof: TTS output is deterministic per input but provider-dependent

rule.rationale: Omitting `provider` from the key serves OpenAI cached audio when the caller requested Google TTS, corrupting downstream assumptions about voice and SSML. Adding provider keeps cache safe across multi-provider routing.

rule.statement: Preprocess text before synthesis: strip Markdown (`#`, `*`, `_`, backticks), strip raw HTML (`<[^>]+>`), expand `$` → ` dollars `, `%` → ` percent `, and spell out one-word abbreviations (`API` → `A-P-I` or `application programming interface`).

rationale.source: TTS listener studies; production audio replay reports

rule.rationale: All TTS providers read `**bold**` as "asterisk asterisk bold asterisk asterisk" and read `API` as "ay" (one syllable). Listeners report this as the single most common quality failure in agent-driven audio.

rule.statement: Cap a single OpenAI TTS call at 4000 characters (not the documented 4096). Reject payloads above the cap and route them to `tts-implementation` LongTextTTS chunking.

rationale.source: OpenAI API client truncation regressions (Python SDK ≤1.30)

rule.rationale: Some client versions silently truncate at exactly 4096 bytes (not chars), cutting the last 1-5 words off multi-byte UTF-8 strings. The 4000 soft cap absorbs the off-by-one without testing every SDK version.

rule.statement: Insert a 1.2-second delay between consecutive OpenAI TTS calls on tier 1 (50 RPM). For tier 2+ accounts, document the tier's documented RPM and compute delay = 60 / RPM seconds.

rationale.source: OpenAI tier limits page; batch failure traces

rule.rationale: 100 short clips synthesized back-to-back without delay hit the tier-1 50 RPM ceiling around clip 50, returning HTTP 429 with no useful retry-after header. A computed delay makes batch jobs predictable.
